commands.repair_reason: - [P2] The PR has a concrete implementation and proof, but the dirty head plus upgrade/session-state tradeoff need rebase and maintainer review rather than an automated repair lane.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain concern found; the diff does not change dependencies, CI, secrets, auth, or downloaded code execution paths. (sha=a68437d57b5fb88eca0d2aa661e0e25ba6e810ba)
commands.review_summary:
This PR changes QMD managed collection names from agent-suffixed identifiers to unscoped names, adds scoped-to-unscoped migration/rebind handling, and updates memory docs, schema help, and regression tests.

PR surface: Source +103, Tests +772, Docs 0. Total +875 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a PR review: this is a refactor/migration proposal, not a reported current-main bug. The behavior difference is source-verifiable because current main still generates scoped QMD names and the PR generates unscoped names.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The PR has a concrete implementation and proof, but the dirty head plus upgrade/session-state tradeoff need rebase and maintainer review rather than an automated repair lane.

target.title: refactor(memory): drop redundant agent-id scoping from qmd collection names
target.body:
## Summary

- **What:** drop the redundant `-<agentId>` scoping from managed qmd collection names. Each agent has its **own isolated qmd index** (`agents/<agentId>/qmd/index.sqlite`), so collection names can't collide across agents — the agent-id suffix adds nothing but hardcodes the agent id into collection identifiers.
- **How:** remove `scopeCollectionBase` (and two now-dead path helpers, `isPathInsideRoot` / `canonicalizePathForContainment`); name default collections by their base (`memory-root`, `memory-dir`), the session collection `sessions`, and extra collections by their explicit name. Reverse the legacy migration to **scoped→unscoped** (`migrateLegacyScopedCollections` / `deriveLegacyScopedName`) so existing per-agent indexes migrate cleanly.
- **Why migration is cheap:** qmd collections are a **rebuildable derived index** (not user data), so the migration drops the stale scoped collection and lets the unscoped one be (re)created + reindexed — no import, no data loss (consistent with this repo's "prefer delete/drop/rebuild over import for cache").
- **Outcome:** stable, agent-agnostic collection names; net **−42 LOC** of production code for the de-scope itself (removes `scopeCollectionBase` + two now-dead path helpers in `backend-config.ts`).
- **Orphan cleanup:** the scoped→unscoped migration is **kind-gated**. Engine-owned collections (`memory`/`sessions`, whose base names are engine-generated and not user-suppliable) are removed even when their pattern has drifted from the current default; user-named (`custom`) collections keep the path+pattern guards and are **never** auto-deleted. Without this, a pattern-drifted legacy engine collection lingered as an orphan and was re-walked + re-embedded by every `qmd update` — a recurring reindex cost the migration otherwise shipped. (Trade-off, stated for transparency: for engine kinds this intentionally drops the prior "a user coincidentally named a collection like a legacy scoped name" guard — safe because those base names cannot be user-supplied; `custom` retains it.)
- **Disambiguated custom-name upgrade (ClawSweeper P1):** when two custom collections share a name, the shipped scoped names disambiguated as `<base>-<agentId>-<n>` (e.g. `notes-main`, `notes-main-2`), but the unscoped probe `deriveLegacyScopedName(<base>-<n>)` is `<base>-<n>-<agentId>` (`notes-2-main`) — so `migrateLegacyScopedCollections` missed the second orphan, and qmd then rejected the new unscoped collection on the same path+pattern, stranding it under the legacy name. `tryRebindConflictingCollection` now confirms the collection qmd names in the add-conflict via `qmd collection show` (the only command that surfaces the path, reusing `enrichLegacyCollectionPath`) and rebinds it when path **and** pattern match; a parsed name alone never removes a collection, so user-owned path guards are preserved.
- **Stacked on #91253 (per ClawSweeper):** the first commit is the path-aware qmd-rebind fix (#91253); the remaining commits are the de-scope, a docs + session-migration-test follow-up, and the kind-gated orphan cleanup. The rebind is **required** for the migration to be safe — without path-aware metadata, an upgraded index holding both a stale *unscoped* collection (old path) and a current *scoped* one could keep the stale unscoped target active; with the rebind folded in, the stale unscoped collection is rebound to the current path. Once #91253 lands, this rebases to drop that first commit.

## Linked context

Closes #91259

Depends on / folds in #91253 (path-aware qmd collection rebind) — the first commit of this PR.

Related: #91265 (an earlier PR toward the same proposal; this one folds in the path-aware rebind so existing scoped indexes migrate safely, adds an upgrade-case regression, and includes live before/after behavior proof).

Requested by a maintainer or owner? No — follows from the proposal in #91259.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: managed qmd collection names dropping the redundant `-<agentId>` suffix; existing per-agent indexes migrate scoped→unscoped; and orphaned engine-owned legacy collections are cleaned up.

Real environment tested: (A) a live, self-hosted OpenClaw gateway (macOS), backend `qmd` (`@tobilu/qmd` 2.5.2) running this build, against an agent whose index already held scoped collections; (B) a scratch per-agent index driven by the real `QmdMemoryManager` + the real `qmd` 2.5.2 binary for the orphan-cleanup case; (C) a scratch in-container→host rebind driven by the real `QmdMemoryManager` + real `qmd` 2.5.2 binary (the sandbox-mode transition).

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```
# (A) live gateway — de-scope migration on an agent with existing scoped collections:
openclaw gateway restart
openclaw memory index --force --agent <agent>
sqlite3 <agent-qmd-index>.sqlite "select name,path from store_collections order by name;"

# (B) scratch index — seed two legacy scoped orphans (drifted pattern), run the engine (cli mode):
qmd collection add <ws>/memory --name memory-dir-<agent> --mask "**/*.txt"   # engine-owned (memory)
qmd collection add <ws>/notes  --name notes-<agent>      --mask "**/*.txt"   # user-owned   (custom)
# then QmdMemoryManager.create({ mode: "cli" }) runs the migration; list collections before/after.

# (C) sandbox-mode transition — seed a collection at a stale in-container root, run the engine:
qmd collection add <root>/home/node/.openclaw/teams/x/workspace --name workspace --mask "**/*.md"
# then QmdMemoryManager.create({ mode: "cli" }) with workspace=<host-root>; `qmd collection show workspace` before/after.
```

Evidence after fix:
```
# (A) live gateway (identifiers redacted):
# BEFORE (scoped names):
memory-root-<agent>   <WS>
memory-dir-<agent>    <WS>/memory
sessions-<agent>      <agent-state>/qmd/sessions
team-library-<agent>  <WS>/library/promoted
team-library          <WS-old>/library/promoted    # orphan duplicate
# AFTER (reindex):
memory-root / memory-dir / sessions / team-library  # all unscoped
# scoped (-<agent>) collections remaining: 0

# (B) scratch index, real qmd 2.5.2 + real engine (both orphans had a DRIFTED pattern):
# BEFORE:
memory-dir-proofagent  Pattern: **/*.txt   # engine-owned (memory)
notes-proofagent       Pattern: **/*.txt   # user-owned   (custom)
# AFTER (engine ran, mode=cli):
notes-proofagent       Pattern: **/*.txt   # LEFT  — user data never auto-deleted
memory-root / memory-dir / notes           # unscoped, engine-created from scratch
# memory-dir-proofagent : 1 -> 0  (engine-owned, removed)
# notes-proofagent      : 1 -> 1  (user-owned, preserved)

# (C) sandbox-mode transition, real qmd 2.5.2 + real engine:
# BEFORE: workspace -> .../home/node/.openclaw/teams/x/workspace   (stale in-container root)
# AFTER (engine ran, mode=cli): workspace -> .../host/workspace    (rebound to host root)
```

Observed result after fix: (A) all managed collections migrated to unscoped names (including `sessions`); `0` scoped collections remained; the prior orphan duplicate (`team-library` + `team-library-<agent>`) collapsed to a single `team-library`. (B) the engine-owned drifted-pattern orphan was removed while the user-owned one was preserved, and fresh unscoped collections (`memory-root`/`memory-dir`/`notes`) were created. (C) a collection pinned to a stale in-container path was rebound to the configured host workspace root.

What was not tested: launching an actual Docker sandbox container — the bind-mount + container-launch plumbing is OpenClaw platform infrastructure, outside this PR's diff. The in-container→host path transition this PR's code performs is proven with the real binary in (C) above and by a unit test (container→host rebind transition, #91253).

Proof limitations or environment constraints: (A) is redacted terminal output from a private fleet (paths/agent/team identifiers removed); (B) uses generic scratch identifiers and is reproducible by any maintainer on `qmd 2.5.2`.

Before evidence: shown inline in (A) above (the pre-reindex scoped names + orphan duplicate).

## Tests and validation

Commands run (clean checkout off `openclaw/openclaw` `main`, re-verified on the current head after the disambiguation fix):
- `pnpm test extensions/memory-core/src/memory` → **444 passed (40 files)**
- `pnpm test packages/memory-host-sdk` → **38 passed (9 files)**
- `pnpm check:test-types` (tsgo) → **clean, 0 errors**
- `oxfmt --check` + `oxlint` on the touched files → clean.

Regression coverage:
- Default + custom collection-name tests now assert the unscoped form.
- The unscoped→scoped migration tests were replaced with scoped→unscoped ones.
- **Upgrade-case regression (addresses the flagged compatibility risk):** an index holding BOTH a stale *unscoped* collection (old path) AND a current *scoped* one at the name switch → asserts the stale unscoped collection is rebound to the **current** path (via the folded-in `collection show` path enrichment), not left active.
- **Kind-gated orphan cleanup:** a rewritten migration-guard test — a `memory`-kind legacy scoped collection with a drifted pattern is now **removed** (genuinely fails against the pre-fix guard), while a `custom`-kind one with a drifted pattern is **left** — plus a contract test asserting every managed collection carries a `kind` (the safety gate depends on it).
- **Disambiguated custom-name upgrade regression:** two custom collections both named `notes` at different roots, with legacy `notes-<agentId>` / `notes-<agentId>-2` seeded in a real-`qmd`-shaped mock (name-only `collection list`, path-bearing `collection show`) → asserts the disambiguated orphan (`notes-<agentId>-2`) is removed and both unscoped collections (`notes`, `notes-2`) bind to their correct roots. Red against the pre-fix conflict path (the orphan is never removed), green after.

Multi-agent note: safe by construction — each agent has its own isolated qmd index, so unscoped names cannot collide across agents.

## Risk checklist

User-visible behavior change? Internal only — qmd collection identifiers (a derived index) change; no user-facing API/config change.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? Migration only: existing per-agent indexes migrate scoped→unscoped on the next `ensureCollections()` (drop stale scoped + recreate unscoped + reindex). No config keys changed.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No.

Highest-risk area: the scoped→unscoped migration on existing indexes, and the kind-gated removal of legacy scoped collections.

How is that risk mitigated: removal only ever targets the exact derived legacy name `<base>-<agentId>` for a **desired** collection. For `custom` collections the path+pattern guards remain, so a user-named collection is never auto-deleted. For `memory`/`sessions` the base names are engine-generated and cannot be user-supplied, so the derived legacy name is unambiguously engine-owned; if such a collection is still configured it is simply re-added at the same path/pattern (net-zero, a reindex). qmd collections are rebuildable, so any dropped-then-recreated collection reindexes from source with no user-visible data loss.

## Current review state

Next action: maintainer review.

What is still waiting: CI + review.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed: ClawSweeper review findings on #91274 (docs example, session-migration coverage, scoped-duplicate upgrade case, orphan cleanup, and the disambiguated custom-name migration P1 — fixed via the show-verified conflict rebind).
target.author: sasan1200

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

target.title: ci: upgrade actions, add least-privilege permissions and concurrency
target.body:
## Summary

A few hardening/maintenance upgrades to `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, found while auditing the workflow:

- **`actions/checkout@v4` → `v6`** and **`actions/setup-node@v4` → `v6`** — both were two majors behind. I checked the v6 changelogs: setup-node v6's only breaking change is to *implicit* cache auto-detection defaults for npm/pnpm/yarn — this workflow already sets `cache: pnpm` explicitly, so it's unaffected.
- **`permissions: contents: read`** — no `permissions` block means the `GITHUB_TOKEN` gets the repo's default permissions (often broader read/write). This workflow only checks out code and runs tests/build, so least-privilege `contents: read` is sufficient.
- **`concurrency` group with `cancel-in-progress: true`** — rapid pushes to the same branch/PR currently queue up redundant full-matrix runs (2 Node versions × full lint/typecheck/test/build pipeline each). This cancels superseded runs to save CI minutes.

This is independent of #6 (the Node 20→22/24 bump), so I split it into its own PR to keep changes atomic.

## Hosted workflow run — live proof (this exact modified file, on GitHub-hosted runners)

GitHub gates first-time fork-contributor workflow runs on this PR behind maintainer approval, but `push` also triggers this workflow (`branches: ["**"]`) — so the **exact byte-for-byte modified workflow file from this branch** already ran end-to-end on GitHub's hosted infrastructure when I pushed it to my fork:

**Run:** https://github.com/joeVenner/vox/actions/runs/27153484253 (commit `7c8c5723f45e`, the current PR head) — **✅ both matrix jobs succeeded**

Copied live output confirming each change actually took effect at runtime:

**1. `permissions: contents: read` — confirmed narrowed in the live job setup:**
```
##[group]GITHUB_TOKEN Permissions
Contents: read
Metadata: read
##[endgroup]
```
(Default unscoped tokens carry many more scopes — this confirms the block is respected by the runner.)

**2. `actions/checkout@v6` — ran and checked out the PR head commit cleanly:**
```
##[group]Run actions/checkout@v6
with:
  repository: joeVenner/vox
  ...
Syncing repository: joeVenner/vox
[command]/usr/bin/git -c protocol.version=2 fetch --no-tags --prune --no-recurse-submodules --depth=1 origin +7c8c5723f45eb5d68441f6c6c953361b523f770d:refs/remotes/origin/chore/upgrade-ci-actions
```

**3. `actions/setup-node@v6` — resolved and cached Node correctly:**
```
##[group]Run actions/setup-node@v6
with:
  node-version: 22
Found in cache @ /opt/hostedtoolcache/node/22.22.3/x64
[warning]This project is configured to use pnpm because /home/runner/work/vox/vox/package.json has a "packageManager" field
```

**4. Full pipeline green on the live runner (identical to what `main`'s CI runs):**
```
> pnpm run lint
> pnpm run format:check && oxlint --deny-warnings src test
All matched files use the correct format.
Finished in 182ms on 23 files using 4 threads.
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
Finished in 11ms on 18 files with 95 rules using 4 threads.

> pnpm run typecheck
> tsgo -p tsconfig.json --noEmit
(clean exit)

> pnpm test
# tests 16
# pass 16
# fail 0

> pnpm run build
ESM dist/cli.js     30.46 KB
⚡️ Build success
```
Job conclusion: `Complete job` → success, both `test (20)` and `test (22)` matrix legs green.

## Local proof (supplementary — confirms the underlying pipeline this workflow invokes is unaffected)

```
$ python3 -c "import yaml; d = yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/ci.yml')); ..."
YAML parses OK
permissions: {'contents': 'read'}
concurrency: {'group': '${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}', 'cancel-in-progress': True}
checkout: ['actions/checkout@v6']
setup-node: ['actions/setup-node@v6']

$ gh api repos/actions/checkout/git/refs/tags/v6 --jq '.ref'
refs/tags/v6
$ gh api repos/actions/setup-node/git/refs/tags/v6 --jq '.ref'
refs/tags/v6

$ pnpm exec oxlint --deny-warnings src test && pnpm run typecheck && pnpm test && pnpm run build
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
# tests 16 / # pass 16 / # fail 0
ESM dist/cli.js  ⚡️ Build success
```
(Run on Node v22.22.3, matching the PR head commit `7c8c5723f45e`.)

Note on the matrix shown in the linked run (`[20, 22]`): this branch was cut from `main` before #6 landed, so it still carries the pre-bump matrix — irrelevant to what's being verified here (the action versions, permissions, and concurrency config), which are matrix-independent and identical to what would run post-merge.

commands.author: joeVenner

target.title: ci: upgrade actions, add least-privilege permissions and concurrency
target.body:
## Summary

A few hardening/maintenance upgrades to `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, found while auditing the workflow:

- **`actions/checkout@v4` → `v6`** and **`actions/setup-node@v4` → `v6`** — both were two majors behind. I checked the v6 changelogs: setup-node v6's only breaking change is to *implicit* cache auto-detection defaults for npm/pnpm/yarn — this workflow already sets `cache: pnpm` explicitly, so it's unaffected.
- **`permissions: contents: read`** — no `permissions` block means the `GITHUB_TOKEN` gets the repo's default permissions (often broader read/write). This workflow only checks out code and runs tests/build, so least-privilege `contents: read` is sufficient.
- **`concurrency` group with `cancel-in-progress: true`** — rapid pushes to the same branch/PR currently queue up redundant full-matrix runs (2 Node versions × full lint/typecheck/test/build pipeline each). This cancels superseded runs to save CI minutes.

This is independent of #6 (the Node 20→22/24 bump), so I split it into its own PR to keep changes atomic.

## Hosted workflow run — live proof (this exact modified file, on GitHub-hosted runners)

GitHub gates first-time fork-contributor workflow runs on this PR behind maintainer approval, but `push` also triggers this workflow (`branches: ["**"]`) — so the **exact byte-for-byte modified workflow file from this branch** already ran end-to-end on GitHub's hosted infrastructure when I pushed it to my fork:

**Run:** https://github.com/joeVenner/vox/actions/runs/27153484253 (commit `7c8c5723f45e`, the current PR head) — **✅ both matrix jobs succeeded**

Copied live output confirming each change actually took effect at runtime:

**1. `permissions: contents: read` — confirmed narrowed in the live job setup:**
```
##[group]GITHUB_TOKEN Permissions
Contents: read
Metadata: read
##[endgroup]
```
(Default unscoped tokens carry many more scopes — this confirms the block is respected by the runner.)

**2. `actions/checkout@v6` — ran and checked out the PR head commit cleanly:**
```
##[group]Run actions/checkout@v6
with:
  repository: joeVenner/vox
  ...
Syncing repository: joeVenner/vox
[command]/usr/bin/git -c protocol.version=2 fetch --no-tags --prune --no-recurse-submodules --depth=1 origin +7c8c5723f45eb5d68441f6c6c953361b523f770d:refs/remotes/origin/chore/upgrade-ci-actions
```

**3. `actions/setup-node@v6` — resolved and cached Node correctly:**
```
##[group]Run actions/setup-node@v6
with:
  node-version: 22
Found in cache @ /opt/hostedtoolcache/node/22.22.3/x64
[warning]This project is configured to use pnpm because /home/runner/work/vox/vox/package.json has a "packageManager" field
```

**4. Full pipeline green on the live runner (identical to what `main`'s CI runs):**
```
> pnpm run lint
> pnpm run format:check && oxlint --deny-warnings src test
All matched files use the correct format.
Finished in 182ms on 23 files using 4 threads.
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
Finished in 11ms on 18 files with 95 rules using 4 threads.

> pnpm run typecheck
> tsgo -p tsconfig.json --noEmit
(clean exit)

> pnpm test
# tests 16
# pass 16
# fail 0

> pnpm run build
ESM dist/cli.js     30.46 KB
⚡️ Build success
```
Job conclusion: `Complete job` → success, both `test (20)` and `test (22)` matrix legs green.

## Local proof (supplementary — confirms the underlying pipeline this workflow invokes is unaffected)

```
$ python3 -c "import yaml; d = yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/ci.yml')); ..."
YAML parses OK
permissions: {'contents': 'read'}
concurrency: {'group': '${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}', 'cancel-in-progress': True}
checkout: ['actions/checkout@v6']
setup-node: ['actions/setup-node@v6']

$ gh api repos/actions/checkout/git/refs/tags/v6 --jq '.ref'
refs/tags/v6
$ gh api repos/actions/setup-node/git/refs/tags/v6 --jq '.ref'
refs/tags/v6

$ pnpm exec oxlint --deny-warnings src test && pnpm run typecheck && pnpm test && pnpm run build
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
# tests 16 / # pass 16 / # fail 0
ESM dist/cli.js  ⚡️ Build success
```
(Run on Node v22.22.3, matching the PR head commit `7c8c5723f45e`.)

Note on the matrix shown in the linked run (`[20, 22]`): this branch was cut from `main` before #6 landed, so it still carries the pre-bump matrix — irrelevant to what's being verified here (the action versions, permissions, and concurrency config), which are matrix-independent and identical to what would run post-merge.

target.title: Ensure exceptions are logged through standard logging mechanism
target.body:
Audit and fix silent exception swallows across the OpenClaw Windows Companion app, and sanitize sensitive details from tray logs. Bare `catch (Exception) { }` and similar patterns now log via IOpenClawLogger, OpenClawTray Services.Logger, or System.Diagnostics.Trace (for self-referential/static contexts) before swallowing or rethrowing. Log messages include type-name prefixes (e.g. `DataModelStore:`, `SettingsPage:`, `ChatWindow:`) for greppability.

Consolidates two related efforts:
- This PR (RBrid: exception logging audit + log-prefix conventions).
- #671 by Christine Yan (tray log sanitization). Merged in as commit `0429f49a` with co-authorship preserved.

## Exception logging

Patterns applied:

- Typed catches with appropriate severity (Error for real bugs, Warn for user-visible degradation, Debug for routine teardown/shutdown/cleanup/dispatcher breadcrumbs).
- Atomic-write catches (McpAuthToken, DeviceIdentity) capture the original exception, Trace, attempt cleanup with its own typed+Traced catch, then rethrow.
- OCE/ODE during shutdown narrowed to Debug to avoid noise.
- Logger.cs self-referential catches use Trace.WriteLine to avoid recursion.
- App.xaml.cs OnProcessExit/LogCrash use belt-and-suspenders Trace + Console.Error with documented `nothing-left-to-call` innermost guards.
- Type-name log prefixes added/restored across audited sites for consistency with existing conventions.

## Log sanitization (from #671)

Centralized in `OpenClaw.Shared.TokenSanitizer` with a mirror in `CommandCenterTextHelper` for tray support context:

- Bearer tokens, API keys, and credential-shaped substrings redacted.
- IPv4 and IPv6 addresses (including bracketed form and RFC 6874 zone identifiers like `eth0.1`, `br-1234`, `wlan_0`) redacted to `<ipv6>` / `<ipv4>`.
- Regex evaluation is timeout-guarded (1s); on timeout, output is replaced with a public `SanitizerTimeoutSentinel` constant so callers fail safe rather than leak.
- Outer try/catch on all sanitizer entry points; sanitizer never throws.
- `IpV6Pattern` / `RedactIfValidIpV6` exposed via `InternalsVisibleTo` so the tray helper reuses the same canonical regex.

## Review

Iterated through six rounds of dual-model adversarial code review (Opus + Codex via the Hanselman skill). All HIGH-consensus findings addressed; final round converged on a single LOW finding both reviewers explicitly recommended accepting as the necessary trade-off for VLAN-style zone identifiers.

## Validation

- `dotnet test OpenClaw.Shared.Tests` — 2083 passed / 29 skipped (3 new sanitizer theory rows for interface name varieties).
- `dotnet test OpenClaw.Tray.Tests` — 938 passed.
- TokenSanitizer suite — 68 passed.
target.author: rbrid
